authors_text: Jasmina Gajcin,Ivana Dusparic
title: ReCCoVER: Detecting Causal Confusion for Explainable Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: ReCoVER：检测可解释强化学习的因果混淆
abstract: Despite notable results in various fields over the recent years, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithms lack transparency, affecting user trust and hindering their deployment to high-risk tasks. Causal confusion refers to a phenomenon where an agent learns spurious correlations between features which might not hold across the entire state space, preventing safe deployment to real tasks where such correlations might be broken. In this work, we examine whether an agent relies on spurious correlations in critical states, and propose an alternative subset of features on which it should base its decisions instead, to make it less susceptible to causal confusion. Our goal is to increase transparency of DRL agents by exposing the influence of learned spurious correlations on its decisions, and offering advice to developers about feature selection in different parts of state space, to avoid causal confusion. We propose ReCCoVER, an algorithm which detects causal confusion in agent's reasoning before deployment, by executing its policy in alternative environments where certain correlations between features do not hold. We demonstrate our approach in taxi and grid world environments, where ReCCoVER detects states in which an agent relies on spurious correlations and offers a set of features that should be considered instead.
summary: Despite notable results in various fields over the recent years, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithms lack transparency, affecting user trust and hindering their deployment to high-risk tasks. Causal confusion refers to a phenomenon where an agent learns spurious correlations between features which might not hold across the entire state space, preventing safe deployment to real tasks where such correlations might be broken. In this work, we examine whether an agent relies on spurious correlations in critical states, and propose an alternative subset of features on which it should base its decisions instead, to make it less susceptible to causal confusion. Our goal is to increase transparency of DRL agents by exposing the influence of learned spurious correlations on its decisions, and offering advice to developers about feature selection in different parts of state space, to avoid causal confusion. We propose ReCCoVER, an algorithm which detects causal confusion in agent's reasoning before deployment, by executing its policy in alternative environments where certain correlations between features do not hold. We demonstrate our approach in taxi and grid world environments, where ReCCoVER detects states in which an agent relies on spurious correlations and offers a set of features that should be considered instead.
abstract_zh: 尽管近年来在各个领域取得了显着的成果，但深度强化学习（DRL）算法缺乏透明度，影响了用户的信任并阻碍了其部署到高风险任务。因果混淆是指代理学习特征之间的虚假相关性的现象，这些相关性可能不适用于整个状态空间，从而阻止安全部署到此类相关性可能被破坏的实际任务。在这项工作中，我们检查智能体是否依赖于关键状态下的虚假相关性，并提出了一个替代的特征子集，它应该以此为基础做出决策，以使其不易受到因果混乱的影响。我们的目标是通过暴露习得的虚假相关性对其决策的影响来提高 DRL 代理的透明度，并向开发人员提供有关状态空间不同部分的特征选择的建议，以避免因果混淆。我们提出了 ReCCoVER，一种算法，通过在特征之间不存在某些相关性的替代环境中执行其策略，在部署之前检测代理推理中的因果混乱。我们在出租车和网格世界环境中展示了我们的方法，其中 ReCCoVER 检测代理依赖虚假相关性的状态，并提供一组应该考虑的功能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ReCCoVER算法，用于检测深度强化学习代理在决策中依赖的虚假相关性，以提高透明度和安全性。
AI.motivation: 深度强化学习缺乏透明度，导致用户信任度低且难以部署到高风险任务中，因为代理可能依赖于状态空间中不稳定的虚假相关性。
AI.method: 通过在替代环境中执行代理策略，验证其是否依赖虚假相关性，并提供替代特征子集以减少因果混淆。
AI.result: 在出租车和网格世界环境中，ReCCoVER成功检测到代理依赖虚假相关性的状态，并推荐了更可靠的特征集合。
AI.conclusion: ReCCoVER能有效暴露代理决策中的因果混淆问题，为开发者提供特征选择建议，提升DRL的可解释性和安全性。

authors_text: T. Takada,W. Shimaya,Y. Ohmura,Y. Kuniyoshi
title: Disentangling Patterns and Transformations from One Sequence of Images with Shape-invariant Lie Group Transformer
title_zh: 使用形状不变李群变换器从一系列图像中解开模式和变换
abstract: An effective way to model the complex real world is to view the world as a composition of basic components of objects and transformations. Although humans through development understand the compositionality of the real world, it is extremely difficult to equip robots with such a learning mechanism. In recent years, there has been significant research on autonomously learning representations of the world using the deep learning; however, most studies have taken a statistical approach, which requires a large number of training data. Contrary to such existing methods, we take a novel algebraic approach for representation learning based on a simpler and more intuitive formulation that the observed world is the combination of multiple independent patterns and transformations that are invariant to the shape of patterns. Since the shape of patterns can be viewed as the invariant features against symmetric transformations such as translation or rotation, we can expect that the patterns can naturally be extracted by expressing transformations with symmetric Lie group transformers and attempting to reconstruct the scene with them. Based on this idea, we propose a model that disentangles the scenes into the minimum number of basic components of patterns and Lie transformations from only one sequence of images, by introducing the learnable shape-invariant Lie group transformers as transformation components. Experiments show that given one sequence of images in which two objects are moving independently, the proposed model can discover the hidden distinct objects and multiple shape-invariant transformations that constitute the scenes.

Contrary to such existing methods, we take a novel algebraic approach for representation learning based on a simpler and more intuitive formulation that the observed world is the combination of multiple independent patterns and transformations that are invariant to the shape of patterns. Since the shape of patterns can be viewed as the invariant features against symmetric transformations such as translation or rotation, we can expect that the patterns can naturally be extracted by expressing transformations with symmetric Lie group transformers and attempting to reconstruct the scene with them. Based on this idea, we propose a model that disentangles the scenes into the minimum number of basic components of patterns and Lie transformations from only one sequence of images, by introducing the learnable shape-invariant Lie group transformers as transformation components. abstract_zh: 对复杂的现实世界进行建模的一种有效方法是将世界视为对象和变换的基本组件的组合。尽管人类通过发展了解现实世界的组成性，但为机器人配备这样的学习机制却极其困难。近年来，利用深度学习自主学习世界表征的研究取得了显着进展。然而，大多数研究都采用统计方法，这需要大量的训练数据。与现有的方法相反，我们采用一种新颖的代数方法进行表示学习，该方法基于更简单、更直观的公式，即观察到的世界是多个独立模式和对模式形状不变的变换的组合。由于图案的形状可以被视为针对平移或旋转等对称变换的不变特征，因此我们可以期望通过使用对称李群变换器表达变换并尝试用它们重建场景来自然地提取图案。基于这个想法，我们提出了一种模型，通过引入可学习的形状不变李群变换器作为变换组件，将场景分解为最少数量的模式基本组件和仅来自一个图像序列的李变换。实验表明，给定两个对象独立移动的一组图像，所提出的模型可以发现隐藏的不同对象和构成场景的多个形状不变变换。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于形状不变李群变换器的模型，从单个图像序列中分离出基本模式和变换。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖统计模型需大量数据，而人类通过组合对象与变换理解世界，需开发更直观的代数方法实现机器人对现实世界的组成结构学习。
AI.method: 引入可学习的形状不变李群变换器，将场景分解为最小数量的基本模式和李变换，利用对称变换不变性提取模式并重构场景。
AI.result: 在仅含两个独立运动物体的图像序列实验中，模型成功发现隐藏物体及多组形状不变变换。
AI.conclusion: 该代数方法能以少量数据有效分离场景中的基本组件，为机器人理解世界组成提供新思路。

authors_text: Klaas Dijkstra,Maya Aghaei,Femke Jaarsma,Martin Dijkstra,Rudy Folkersma,Jan Jager,Jaap van de Loosdrecht
title: On the Effect of Pre-Processing and Model Complexity for Plastic Analysis Using Short-Wave-Infrared Hyper-Spectral Imaging
title_zh: 利用短波红外高光谱成像进行塑性分析的预处理和模型复杂性的影响
abstract: The importance of plastic waste recycling is undeniable. In this respect, computer vision and deep learning enable solutions through the automated analysis of short-wave-infrared hyper-spectral images of plastics. In this paper, we offer an exhaustive empirical study to show the importance of efficient model selection for resolving the task of hyper-spectral image segmentation of various plastic flakes using deep learning. We assess the complexity level of generic and specialized models and infer their performance capacity: generic models are often unnecessarily complex. We introduce two variants of a specialized hyper-spectral architecture, PlasticNet, that outperforms several well-known segmentation architectures in both performance as well as computational complexity. In addition, we shed lights on the significance of signal pre-processing within the realm of hyper-spectral imaging. To complete our contribution, we introduce the largest, most versatile hyper-spectral dataset of plastic flakes of four primary polymer types.
summary: The importance of plastic waste recycling is undeniable. In this respect, computer vision and deep learning enable solutions through the automated analysis of short-wave-infrared hyper-spectral images of plastics. In this paper, we offer an exhaustive empirical study to show the importance of efficient model selection for resolving the task of hyper-spectral image segmentation of various plastic flakes using deep learning. We assess the complexity level of generic and specialized models and infer their performance capacity: generic models are often unnecessarily complex. We introduce two variants of a specialized hyper-spectral architecture, PlasticNet, that outperforms several well-known segmentation architectures in both performance as well as computational complexity. In addition, we shed lights on the significance of signal pre-processing within the realm of hyper-spectral imaging. To complete our contribution, we introduce the largest, most versatile hyper-spectral dataset of plastic flakes of four primary polymer types.
abstract_zh: 塑料废物回收的重要性是不可否认的。在这方面，计算机视觉和深度学习通过对塑料的短波红外高光谱图像进行自动分析来提供解决方案。在本文中，我们提供了详尽的实证研究，以证明有效模型选择对于使用深度学习解决各种塑料薄片的高光谱图像分割任务的重要性。我们评估通用模型和专用模型的复杂程度，并推断它们的性能能力：通用模型通常不必要地复杂。我们引入了专门的超光谱架构 PlasticNet 的两种变体，它在性能和计算复杂性方面都优于几种众所周知的分割架构。此外，我们还阐明了高光谱成像领域信号预处理的重要性。为了完成我们的贡献，我们引入了四种主要聚合物类型的塑料薄片的最大、最通用的高光谱数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了短波红外高光谱成像在塑料分析中的预处理效果及模型复杂度影响，提出专用模型PlasticNet并构建了最大的塑料碎片高光谱数据集。
AI.motivation: 塑料废物回收至关重要，但现有通用模型复杂度高且效率不足，缺乏针对高光谱图像分割的专用研究及多样化数据集。
AI.method: 通过实证研究对比通用与专用模型复杂度，设计两种PlasticNet架构，分析预处理对高光谱成像的影响，并构建包含四种主要聚合物类型的塑料碎片高光谱数据集。
AI.result: PlasticNet在分割性能和计算复杂度上优于主流架构，新数据集成为当前最大且最通用的塑料高光谱数据集。
AI.conclusion: 专用模型设计与预处理优化可显著提升高光谱塑料分析效率，新数据集为后续研究提供重要基础。

authors_text: Soufia Bennai,Kamala Amroun,Samir Loudni,Abdelkader Ouali
title: An efficient heuristic approach combining maximal itemsets and area measure for compressing voluminous table constraints
title_zh: 一种有效的启发式方法，结合最大项集和面积测量来压缩大量表约束
abstract: Constraint Programming is a powerful paradigm to model and solve combinatorial problems. While there are many kinds of constraints, the table constraint is perhaps the most significant-being the most well-studied and has the ability to encode any other constraints defined on finite variables. However, constraints can be very voluminous and their size can grow exponentially with their arity. To reduce space and the time complexity, researchers have focused on various forms of compression. In this paper we propose a new approach based on maximal frequent itemsets technique and area measure for enumerating the maximal frequent itemsets relevant for compressing table constraints. Our experimental results show the effectiveness and efficiency of this approach on compression and on solving compressed table constraints.
summary: Constraint Programming is a powerful paradigm to model and solve combinatorial problems. While there are many kinds of constraints, the table constraint is perhaps the most significant-being the most well-studied and has the ability to encode any other constraints defined on finite variables. However, constraints can be very voluminous and their size can grow exponentially with their arity. To reduce space and the time complexity, researchers have focused on various forms of compression. In this paper we propose a new approach based on maximal frequent itemsets technique and area measure for enumerating the maximal frequent itemsets relevant for compressing table constraints. Our experimental results show the effectiveness and efficiency of this approach on compression and on solving compressed table constraints.
abstract_zh: 约束编程是建模和解决组合问题的强大范例。虽然约束有很多种，但表约束可能是最重要的——研究得最多，并且能够对有限变量上定义的任何其他约束进行编码。然而，约束可能非常庞大，并且它们的大小会随着数量的增加而呈指数级增长。为了降低空间和时间复杂度，研究人员关注于各种形式的压缩。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于最大频繁项集技术和面积度量的新方法，用于枚举与压缩表约束相关的最大频繁项集。我们的实验结果表明了这种方法在压缩和解决压缩表约束方面的有效性和效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合最大频繁项集和面积度量的启发式方法，用于压缩大规模表约束。
AI.motivation: 表约束在约束编程中至关重要，但其体积可能随变量数量指数级增长，导致存储和计算效率低下，亟需高效压缩方法。
AI.method: 基于最大频繁项集技术与面积度量，枚举与压缩相关的最大频繁项集，实现表约束的高效压缩。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在压缩效果及压缩后约束求解效率方面均表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法为表约束压缩提供了有效且高效的解决方案，兼顾压缩率与求解性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种结合最大频繁项集和面积度量的启发式方法，用于压缩大规模表约束。
ai_content.motivation: 表约束在约束编程中至关重要，但其体积可能随变量数量指数级增长，导致存储和计算效率低下，亟需高效压缩方法。

search_text: An efficient heuristic approach combining maximal itemsets and area measure for compressing voluminous table constraints 一种有效的启发式方法，结合最大项集和面积测量来压缩大量表约束 Constraint Programming is a powerful paradigm to model and solve combinatorial problems. While there are many kinds of constraints, the table constraint is perhaps the most significant-being the most well-studied and has the ability to encode any other constraints defined on finite variables. However, constraints can be very voluminous and their size can grow exponentially with their arity. To reduce space and the time complexity, researchers have focused on various forms of compression. In this paper we propose a new approach based on maximal frequent itemsets technique and area measure for enumerating the maximal frequent itemsets relevant for compressing table constraints. Our experimental results show the effectiveness and efficiency of this approach on compression and on solving compressed table constraints. 约束编程是建模和解决组合问题的强大范例。虽然约束有很多种，但表约束可能是最重要的——研究得最多，并且能够对有限变量上定义的任何其他约束进行编码。然而，约束可能非常庞大，并且它们的大小会随着数量的增加而呈指数级增长。为了降低空间和时间复杂度，研究人员关注于各种形式的压缩。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于最大频繁项集技术和面积度量的新方法，用于枚举与压缩表约束相关的最大频繁项集。我们的实验结果表明了这种方法在压缩和解决压缩表约束方面的有效性和效率。 本文提出一种结合最大频繁项集和面积度量的启发式方法，用于压缩大规模表约束。 Soufia Bennai,Kamala Amroun,Samir Loudni,Abdelkader Ouali cs.DB,cs.AI

authors_text: James Spall,Xianxin Guo,A. I. Lvovsky
title: Hybrid training of optical neural networks
title_zh: 光学神经网络的混合训练
abstract: Optical neural networks are emerging as a promising type of machine learning hardware capable of energy-efficient, parallel computation. Today's optical neural networks are mainly developed to perform optical inference after in silico training on digital simulators. However, various physical imperfections that cannot be accurately modelled may lead to the notorious reality gap between the digital simulator and the physical system. To address this challenge, we demonstrate hybrid training of optical neural networks where the weight matrix is trained with neuron activation functions computed optically via forward propagation through the network. We examine the efficacy of hybrid training with three different networks: an optical linear classifier, a hybrid opto-electronic network, and a complex-valued optical network. We perform a comparative study to in silico training, and our results show that hybrid training is robust against different kinds of static noise. Our platform-agnostic hybrid training scheme can be applied to a wide variety of optical neural networks, and this work paves the way towards advanced all-optical training in machine intelligence.
summary: Optical neural networks are emerging as a promising type of machine learning hardware capable of energy-efficient, parallel computation. Today's optical neural networks are mainly developed to perform optical inference after in silico training on digital simulators. However, various physical imperfections that cannot be accurately modelled may lead to the notorious reality gap between the digital simulator and the physical system. To address this challenge, we demonstrate hybrid training of optical neural networks where the weight matrix is trained with neuron activation functions computed optically via forward propagation through the network. We examine the efficacy of hybrid training with three different networks: an optical linear classifier, a hybrid opto-electronic network, and a complex-valued optical network. We perform a comparative study to in silico training, and our results show that hybrid training is robust against different kinds of static noise. Our platform-agnostic hybrid training scheme can be applied to a wide variety of optical neural networks, and this work paves the way towards advanced all-optical training in machine intelligence.
abstract_zh: 光学神经网络正在成为一种有前途的机器学习硬件，能够进行节能、并行计算。如今的光学神经网络主要是为了在数字模拟器上进行计算机模拟训练后执行光学推理而开发的。然而，无法准确建模的各种物理缺陷可能会导致数字模拟器与物理系统之间存在臭名昭著的现实差距。为了应对这一挑战，我们演示了光学神经网络的混合训练，其中权重矩阵使用通过网络前向传播光学计算的神经元激活函数进行训练。我们研究了三种不同网络混合训练的效果：光学线性分类器、混合光电网络和复值光学网络。我们对计算机训练进行了比较研究，结果表明混合训练对于不同类型的静态噪声具有鲁棒性。我们的与平台无关的混合训练方案可以应用于各种光学神经网络，这项工作为机器智能领域的高级全光学训练铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 提出一种混合训练方法，解决光学神经网络中的现实差距问题，通过光学前向传播计算激活函数以提高训练效果。
AI.motivation: 现有光学神经网络依赖数字模拟训练，但物理缺陷导致模拟与实际系统间存在性能差距，需通过混合训练提升鲁棒性。
AI.method: 采用混合训练方案，在训练权重矩阵时利用光学前向传播计算激活函数，并通过三种不同网络（光学线性分类器、混合光电网络、复值光学网络）进行实验验证。
AI.result: 实验显示混合训练在多种静态噪声环境下表现优于传统数字训练，且适用于多种光学神经网络架构。
AI.conclusion: 提出的平台无关混合训练方法可广泛应用于各类光学神经网络，为实现全光学机器智能训练奠定基础。

authors_text: Binh T. Dao,Thang V. Nguyen,Hieu H. Pham,Ha Q. Nguyen
title: Phase Recognition in Contrast-Enhanced CT Scans based on Deep Learning and Random Sampling
title_zh: 基于深度学习和随机采样的增强 CT 扫描相位识别
comment: Accepted for publication by Medical Physics
comments: Accepted for publication by Medical Physics
abstract: A fully automated system for interpreting abdominal computed tomography (CT) scans with multiple phases of contrast enhancement requires an accurate classification of the phases. This work aims at developing and validating a precise, fast multi-phase classifier to recognize three main types of contrast phases in abdominal CT scans. We propose in this study a novel method that uses a random sampling mechanism on top of deep CNNs for the phase recognition of abdominal CT scans of four different phases: non-contrast, arterial, venous, and others. The CNNs work as a slice-wise phase prediction, while the random sampling selects input slices for the CNN models. Afterward, majority voting synthesizes the slice-wise results of the CNNs, to provide the final prediction at scan level. Our classifier was trained on 271,426 slices from 830 phase-annotated CT scans, and when combined with majority voting on 30% of slices randomly chosen from each scan, achieved a mean F1-score of 92.09% on our internal test set of 358 scans. The proposed method was also evaluated on 2 external test sets: CTPAC-CCRCC (N = 242) and LiTS (N = 131), which were annotated by our experts. Although a drop in performance has been observed, the model performance remained at a high level of accuracy with a mean F1-score of 76.79% and 86.94% on CTPAC-CCRCC and LiTS datasets, respectively. Our experimental results also showed that the proposed method significantly outperformed the state-of-the-art 3D approaches while requiring less computation time for inference.
summary: A fully automated system for interpreting abdominal computed tomography (CT) scans with multiple phases of contrast enhancement requires an accurate classification of the phases. This work aims at developing and validating a precise, fast multi-phase classifier to recognize three main types of contrast phases in abdominal CT scans. We propose in this study a novel method that uses a random sampling mechanism on top of deep CNNs for the phase recognition of abdominal CT scans of four different phases: non-contrast, arterial, venous, and others. The CNNs work as a slice-wise phase prediction, while the random sampling selects input slices for the CNN models. Afterward, majority voting synthesizes the slice-wise results of the CNNs, to provide the final prediction at scan level. Our classifier was trained on 271,426 slices from 830 phase-annotated CT scans, and when combined with majority voting on 30% of slices randomly chosen from each scan, achieved a mean F1-score of 92.09% on our internal test set of 358 scans. The proposed method was also evaluated on 2 external test sets: CTPAC-CCRCC (N = 242) and LiTS (N = 131), which were annotated by our experts. Although a drop in performance has been observed, the model performance remained at a high level of accuracy with a mean F1-score of 76.79% and 86.94% on CTPAC-CCRCC and LiTS datasets, respectively. Our experimental results also showed that the proposed method significantly outperformed the state-of-the-art 3D approaches while requiring less computation time for inference.
abstract_zh: 用于解释具有多个对比增强阶段的腹部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描的全自动系统需要对阶段进行准确分类。这项工作旨在开发和验证一种精确、快速的多相分类器，以识别腹部 CT 扫描中三种主要类型的对比相。我们在这项研究中提出了一种新颖的方法，该方法在深度 CNN 之上使用随机采样机制来对四个不同阶段的腹部 CT 扫描进行阶段识别：非造影、动脉、静脉等。 CNN 用作切片相位预测，而随机采样则为 CNN 模型选择输入切片。然后，多数投票综合 CNN 的切片结果，以提供扫描级别的最终预测。我们的分类器接受了来自 830 个相位注释 CT 扫描的 271,426 个切片的训练，结合对每次扫描中随机选择的 30% 切片的多数投票，在我们的 358 个扫描的内部测试集上实现了 92.09% 的平均 F1 分数。所提出的方法还在 2 个外部测试集上进行了评估：CTPAC-CCRCC (N = 242) 和 LiTS (N = 131)，并由我们的专家进行了注释。尽管观察到性能下降，但模型性能仍然保持较高的准确度，在 CTPAC-CCRCC 和 LiTS 数据集上的平均 F1 分数分别为 76.79% 和 86.94%。我们的实验结果还表明，所提出的方法显着优于最先进的 3D 方法，同时需要更少的推理计算时间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习和随机采样的方法，用于自动识别腹部增强CT扫描中的四个对比相位（非对比、动脉、静脉和其他）。
AI.motivation: 准确分类腹部CT扫描的对比相位对临床诊断至关重要，但现有方法在处理多相位分类时存在精度和效率不足的问题。
AI.method: 结合深度CNN进行逐片相位预测，并通过随机采样机制选择输入切片，最终采用多数投票法合成结果以实现扫描级预测。
AI.result: 在内部测试集上达到92.09%的平均F1分数，在两个外部数据集上分别达到76.79%和86.94%，且推理时间显著优于现有3D方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保持高精度的同时降低了计算成本，验证了随机采样与深度学习结合在医学影像分析中的有效性。

Our experimental results also showed that the proposed method significantly outperformed the state-of-the-art 3D approaches while requiring less computation time for inference. 用于解释具有多个对比增强阶段的腹部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描的全自动系统需要对阶段进行准确分类。这项工作旨在开发和验证一种精确、快速的多相分类器，以识别腹部 CT 扫描中三种主要类型的对比相。我们在这项研究中提出了一种新颖的方法，该方法在深度 CNN 之上使用随机采样机制来对四个不同阶段的腹部 CT 扫描进行阶段识别：非造影、动脉、静脉等。 CNN 用作切片相位预测，而随机采样则为 CNN 模型选择输入切片。然后，多数投票综合 CNN 的切片结果，以提供扫描级别的最终预测。我们的分类器接受了来自 830 个相位注释 CT 扫描的 271,426 个切片的训练，结合对每次扫描中随机选择的 30% 切片的多数投票，在我们的 358 个扫描的内部测试集上实现了 92.09% 的平均 F1 分数。所提出的方法还在 2 个外部测试集上进行了评估：CTPAC-CCRCC (N = 242) 和 LiTS (N = 131)，并由我们的专家进行了注释。尽管观察到性能下降，但模型性能仍然保持较高的准确度，在 CTPAC-CCRCC 和 LiTS 数据集上的平均 F1 分数分别为 76.79% 和 86.94%。我们的实验结果还表明，所提出的方法显着优于最先进的 3D 方法，同时需要更少的推理计算时间。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习和随机采样的方法，用于自动识别腹部增强CT扫描中的四个对比相位（非对比、动脉、静脉和其他）。 Binh T. Dao,Thang V. Nguyen,Hieu H. Pham,Ha Q. Nguyen eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Hieu T. Nguyen,Ha Q. Nguyen,Hieu H. Pham,Khanh Lam,Linh T. Le,Minh Dao,Van Vu
title: VinDr-Mammo: A large-scale benchmark dataset for computer-aided diagnosis in full-field digital mammography
title_zh: VinDr-Mammo：全视野数字乳腺 X 线摄影计算机辅助诊断的大规模基准数据集
comment: The manuscript is accepted for publication by Scientific Data (Nature)
comments: The manuscript is accepted for publication by Scientific Data (Nature)
abstract: Mammography, or breast X-ray, is the most widely used imaging modality to detect cancer and other breast diseases. Recent studies have shown that deep learning-based computer-assisted detection and diagnosis (CADe or CADx) tools have been developed to support physicians and improve the accuracy of interpreting mammography. However, most published datasets of mammography are either limited on sample size or digitalized from screen-film mammography (SFM), hindering the development of CADe and CADx tools which are developed based on full-field digital mammography (FFDM). To overcome this challenge, we introduce VinDr-Mammo - a new benchmark dataset of FFDM for detecting and diagnosing breast cancer and other diseases in mammography. The dataset consists of 5,000 mammography exams, each of which has four standard views and is double read with disagreement (if any) being resolved by arbitration. It is created for the assessment of Breast Imaging Reporting and Data System (BI-RADS) and density at the breast level. In addition, the dataset also provides the category, location, and BI-RADS assessment of non-benign findings. We make VinDr-Mammo publicly available on PhysioNet as a new imaging resource to promote advances in developing CADe and CADx tools for breast cancer screening.
summary: Mammography, or breast X-ray, is the most widely used imaging modality to detect cancer and other breast diseases. Recent studies have shown that deep learning-based computer-assisted detection and diagnosis (CADe or CADx) tools have been developed to support physicians and improve the accuracy of interpreting mammography. However, most published datasets of mammography are either limited on sample size or digitalized from screen-film mammography (SFM), hindering the development of CADe and CADx tools which are developed based on full-field digital mammography (FFDM). To overcome this challenge, we introduce VinDr-Mammo - a new benchmark dataset of FFDM for detecting and diagnosing breast cancer and other diseases in mammography. The dataset consists of 5,000 mammography exams, each of which has four standard views and is double read with disagreement (if any) being resolved by arbitration. It is created for the assessment of Breast Imaging Reporting and Data System (BI-RADS) and density at the breast level. In addition, the dataset also provides the category, location, and BI-RADS assessment of non-benign findings. We make VinDr-Mammo publicly available on PhysioNet as a new imaging resource to promote advances in developing CADe and CADx tools for breast cancer screening.
abstract_zh: 乳房X光检查或乳腺X射线检查是检测癌症和其他乳腺疾病最广泛使用的成像方式。最近的研究表明，基于深度学习的计算机辅助检测和诊断（CADe 或 CADx）工具已被开发出来，可以为医生提供支持并提高解读乳房 X 光检查的准确性。然而，大多数已发布的乳腺X线摄影数据集要么受到样本大小的限制，要么从屏幕胶片乳腺X线摄影（SFM）数字化，阻碍了基于全场数字乳腺X线摄影（FFDM）开发的CADe和CADx工具的开发。为了克服这一挑战，我们引入了 VinDr-Mammo——一个新的 FFDM 基​​准数据集，用于在乳房 X 光检查中检测和诊断乳腺癌和其他疾病。该数据集由 5,000 个乳房 X 光检查组成，每个检查都有四个标准视图，并经过双重读取，并通过仲裁解决分歧（如果有）。它是为评估乳房成像报告和数据系统 (BI-RADS) 以及乳房水平的密度而创建的。此外，该数据集还提供非良性发现的类别、位置和 BI-RADS 评估。我们在 PhysioNet 上公开提供 VinDr-Mammo，作为一种新的成像资源，以促进乳腺癌筛查 CADe 和 CADx 工具开发的进步。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为VinDr-Mammo的大规模全视野数字乳腺X线摄影（FFDM）基准数据集，用于支持计算机辅助诊断（CADe/CADx）工具的开发。
AI.motivation: 现有乳腺X线数据集样本量有限或基于过时的屏片摄影技术，难以满足基于FFDM的CADe/CADx工具研发需求。
AI.method: 收集5000例包含四标准视图的FFDM影像，通过双人读片并仲裁解决分歧，标注BI-RADS分类、密度及非良性病变信息。
AI.result: 构建包含5000例高质量标注数据的公开数据集，通过PhysioNet平台发布，提供乳腺影像分析标准化评估资源。
AI.conclusion: VinDr-Mammo数据集将推动乳腺癌筛查中CADe/CADx技术的发展，促进AI辅助诊断算法的评估与优化。

authors_text: Jie Pan,Jingwei Huang,Gengdong Cheng,Yong Zeng
title: Reinforcement learning for automatic quadrilateral mesh generation: a soft actor-critic approach
title_zh: 自动四边形网格生成的强化学习：一种软演员批评家方法
abstract: This paper proposes, implements, and evaluates a reinforcement learning (RL)-based computational framework for automatic mesh generation. Mesh generation plays a fundamental role in numerical simulations in the area of computer aided design and engineering (CAD/E). It is identified as one of the critical issues in the NASA CFD Vision 2030 Study. Existing mesh generation methods suffer from high computational complexity, low mesh quality in complex geometries, and speed limitations. These methods and tools, including commercial software packages, are typically semiautomatic and they need inputs or help from human experts. By formulating the mesh generation as a Markov decision process (MDP) problem, we are able to use a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm called "soft actor-critic" to automatically learn from trials the policy of actions for mesh generation. The implementation of this RL algorithm for mesh generation allows us to build a fully automatic mesh generation system without human intervention and any extra clean-up operations, which fills the gap in the existing mesh generation tools. In the experiments to compare with two representative commercial software packages, our system demonstrates promising performance with respect to scalability, generalizability, and effectiveness.
summary: This paper proposes, implements, and evaluates a reinforcement learning (RL)-based computational framework for automatic mesh generation. Mesh generation plays a fundamental role in numerical simulations in the area of computer aided design and engineering (CAD/E). It is identified as one of the critical issues in the NASA CFD Vision 2030 Study. Existing mesh generation methods suffer from high computational complexity, low mesh quality in complex geometries, and speed limitations. These methods and tools, including commercial software packages, are typically semiautomatic and they need inputs or help from human experts. By formulating the mesh generation as a Markov decision process (MDP) problem, we are able to use a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm called "soft actor-critic" to automatically learn from trials the policy of actions for mesh generation. The implementation of this RL algorithm for mesh generation allows us to build a fully automatic mesh generation system without human intervention and any extra clean-up operations, which fills the gap in the existing mesh generation tools. In the experiments to compare with two representative commercial software packages, our system demonstrates promising performance with respect to scalability, generalizability, and effectiveness.
abstract_zh: 本文提出、实现并评估了一种基于强化学习 (RL) 的自动网格生成计算框架。网格生成在计算机辅助设计和工程 (CAD/E) 领域的数值模拟中发挥着基础作用。它被确定为 NASA CFD 2030 年愿景研究中的关键问题之一。现有的网格生成方法存在计算复杂度高、复杂几何形状的网格质量低以及速度限制等问题。这些方法和工具，包括商业软件包，通常是半自动的，需要人类专家的输入或帮助。通过将网格生成表述为马尔可夫决策过程 (MDP) 问题，我们能够使用最先进的强化学习 (RL) 算法（称为“软演员批评家”）从试验中自动学习网格生成的操作策略。这种用于网格生成的强化学习算法的实现使我们能够构建一个全自动网格生成系统，无需人工干预和任何额外的清理操作，这填补了现有网格生成工具的空白。在与两个代表性商业软件包进行比较的实验中，我们的系统在可扩展性、通用性和有效性方面表现出了良好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的自动四边形网格生成框架，采用Soft Actor-Critic算法实现无需人工干预的全自动网格生成。
AI.motivation: 现有网格生成方法存在计算复杂度高、复杂几何体质量差、速度慢及依赖人工干预等问题，难以满足NASA CFD Vision 2030对高效自动化的迫切需求。
AI.method: 将网格生成建模为马尔可夫决策过程（MDP），采用Soft Actor-Critic强化学习算法学习网格生成策略，构建无需人工干预的全自动系统。
AI.result: 实验表明该系统在可扩展性、泛化性和有效性方面优于两类商业软件，实现了复杂几何体的高质量自动网格生成。
AI.conclusion: 本方法填补了现有工具的自动化空白，为工程仿真提供了高效解决方案，未来需进一步验证其在更复杂场景中的性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的自动四边形网格生成框架，采用Soft Actor-Critic算法实现无需人工干预的全自动网格生成。

By formulating the mesh generation as a Markov decision process (MDP) problem, we are able to use a state-of-the-art reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm called "soft actor-critic" to automatically learn from trials the policy of actions for mesh generation. The implementation of this RL algorithm for mesh generation allows us to build a fully automatic mesh generation system without human intervention and any extra clean-up operations, which fills the gap in the existing mesh generation tools. In the experiments to compare with two representative commercial software packages, our system demonstrates promising performance with respect to scalability, generalizability, and effectiveness. 本文提出、实现并评估了一种基于强化学习 (RL) 的自动网格生成计算框架。网格生成在计算机辅助设计和工程 (CAD/E) 领域的数值模拟中发挥着基础作用。它被确定为 NASA CFD 2030 年愿景研究中的关键问题之一。现有的网格生成方法存在计算复杂度高、复杂几何形状的网格质量低以及速度限制等问题。这些方法和工具，包括商业软件包，通常是半自动的，需要人类专家的输入或帮助。通过将网格生成表述为马尔可夫决策过程 (MDP) 问题，我们能够使用最先进的强化学习 (RL) 算法（称为“软演员批评家”）从试验中自动学习网格生成的操作策略。这种用于网格生成的强化学习算法的实现使我们能够构建一个全自动网格生成系统，无需人工干预和任何额外的清理操作，这填补了现有网格生成工具的空白。在与两个代表性商业软件包进行比较的实验中，我们的系统在可扩展性、通用性和有效性方面表现出了良好的性能。 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的自动四边形网格生成框架，采用Soft Actor-Critic算法实现无需人工干预的全自动网格生成。 Jie Pan,Jingwei Huang,Gengdong Cheng,Yong Zeng cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CG

authors_text: Haoze Wu,Aleksandar Zeljić,Guy Katz,Clark Barrett
title: Efficient Neural Network Analysis with Sum-of-Infeasibilities
title_zh: 具有不可行性总和的高效神经网络分析
abstract: Inspired by sum-of-infeasibilities methods in convex optimization, we propose a novel procedure for analyzing verification queries on neural networks with piecewise-linear activation functions. Given a convex relaxation which over-approximates the non-convex activation functions, we encode the violations of activation functions as a cost function and optimize it with respect to the convex relaxation. The cost function, referred to as the Sum-of-Infeasibilities (SoI), is designed so that its minimum is zero and achieved only if all the activation functions are satisfied. We propose a stochastic procedure, DeepSoI, to efficiently minimize the SoI. An extension to a canonical case-analysis-based complete search procedure can be achieved by replacing the convex procedure executed at each search state with DeepSoI. Extending the complete search with DeepSoI achieves multiple simultaneous goals: 1) it guides the search towards a counter-example; 2) it enables more informed branching decisions; and 3) it creates additional opportunities for bound derivation. An extensive evaluation across different benchmarks and solvers demonstrates the benefit of the proposed techniques. In particular, we demonstrate that SoI significantly improves the performance of an existing complete search procedure. Moreover, the SoI-based implementation outperforms other state-of-the-art complete verifiers. We also show that our technique can efficiently improve upon the perturbation bound derived by a recent adversarial attack algorithm.
summary: Inspired by sum-of-infeasibilities methods in convex optimization, we propose a novel procedure for analyzing verification queries on neural networks with piecewise-linear activation functions. Given a convex relaxation which over-approximates the non-convex activation functions, we encode the violations of activation functions as a cost function and optimize it with respect to the convex relaxation. The cost function, referred to as the Sum-of-Infeasibilities (SoI), is designed so that its minimum is zero and achieved only if all the activation functions are satisfied. We propose a stochastic procedure, DeepSoI, to efficiently minimize the SoI. An extension to a canonical case-analysis-based complete search procedure can be achieved by replacing the convex procedure executed at each search state with DeepSoI. Extending the complete search with DeepSoI achieves multiple simultaneous goals: 1) it guides the search towards a counter-example; 2) it enables more informed branching decisions; and 3) it creates additional opportunities for bound derivation. An extensive evaluation across different benchmarks and solvers demonstrates the benefit of the proposed techniques. In particular, we demonstrate that SoI significantly improves the performance of an existing complete search procedure. Moreover, the SoI-based implementation outperforms other state-of-the-art complete verifiers. We also show that our technique can efficiently improve upon the perturbation bound derived by a recent adversarial attack algorithm.
abstract_zh: 受凸优化中不可行性和方法的启发，我们提出了一种新的程序，用于分析具有分段线性激活函数的神经网络上的验证查询。给定过度逼近非凸激活函数的凸松弛，我们将激活函数的违规编码为成本函数，并针对凸松弛对其进行优化。成本函数称为不可行性总和 (SoI)，其最小值为零，并且仅在满足所有激活函数时才实现。我们提出了一种随机过程 DeepSoI，以有效地最小化 SoI。通过用 DeepSoI 替换在每个搜索状态下执行的凸过程，可以实现对基于规范案例分析的完整搜索过程的扩展。使用 DeepSoI 扩展完整搜索可同时实现多个目标：1）它引导搜索走向反例； 2）它可以做出更明智的分支决策； 3）它为有界推导创造了额外的机会。对不同基准和求解器的广泛评估证明了所提出技术的优点。特别是，我们证明 SoI 显着提高了现有完整搜索过程的性能。此外，基于 SoI 的实现优于其他最先进的完整验证器。我们还表明，我们的技术可以有效地改进由最近的对抗性攻击算法得出的扰动界限。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于求和不可行性（SoI）的新方法，用于高效分析具有分段线性激活函数的神经网络验证查询。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络验证方法在处理非凸激活函数时效率低下，难以兼顾精度与计算成本，需更高效的分析框架。
AI.method: 通过凸松弛近似非凸激活函数，将激活函数的违反情况编码为SoI代价函数，并用随机优化算法DeepSoI最小化该函数；结合完全搜索方法提升分支决策与边界推导效率。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中，SoI方法显著提升完整搜索程序性能，优于现有最先进验证器，并改进对抗攻击算法的扰动界。
AI.conclusion: SoI方法为神经网络验证提供了高效且精确的解决方案，兼顾计算效率与验证完整性。

authors_text: Jie Chen,Shouzhen Chen,Junbin Gao,Zengfeng Huang,Junping Zhang,Jian Pu
title: Exploiting Neighbor Effect: Conv-Agnostic GNNs Framework for Graphs with Heterophily
title_zh: 利用邻居效应：异质图的与卷积无关的 GNN 框架
abstract: Due to the homophily assumption in graph convolution networks (GNNs), a common consensus in the graph node classification task is that GNNs perform well on homophilic graphs but may fail on heterophilic graphs with many inter-class edges. However, the previous inter-class edges perspective and related homo-ratio metrics cannot well explain the GNNs performance under some heterophilic datasets, which implies that not all the inter-class edges are harmful to GNNs. In this work, we propose a new metric based on von Neumann entropy to re-examine the heterophily problem of GNNs and investigate the feature aggregation of inter-class edges from an entire neighbor identifiable perspective. Moreover, we propose a simple yet effective Conv-Agnostic GNN framework (CAGNNs) to enhance the performance of most GNNs on heterophily datasets by learning the neighbor effect for each node. Specifically, we first decouple the feature of each node into the discriminative feature for downstream tasks and the aggregation feature for graph convolution. Then, we propose a shared mixer module to adaptively evaluate the neighbor effect of each node to incorporate the neighbor information. The proposed framework can be regarded as a plug-in component and is compatible with most GNNs. The experimental results over nine well-known benchmark datasets indicate that our framework can significantly improve performance, especially for the heterophily graphs. The average performance gain is 9.81%, 25.81%, and 20.61% compared with GIN, GAT, and GCN, respectively. Extensive ablation studies and robustness analysis further verify the effectiveness, robustness, and interpretability of our framework. Code is available at https://github.com/JC-202/CAGNN.
summary: Due to the homophily assumption in graph convolution networks (GNNs), a common consensus in the graph node classification task is that GNNs perform well on homophilic graphs but may fail on heterophilic graphs with many inter-class edges. However, the previous inter-class edges perspective and related homo-ratio metrics cannot well explain the GNNs performance under some heterophilic datasets, which implies that not all the inter-class edges are harmful to GNNs. In this work, we propose a new metric based on von Neumann entropy to re-examine the heterophily problem of GNNs and investigate the feature aggregation of inter-class edges from an entire neighbor identifiable perspective. Moreover, we propose a simple yet effective Conv-Agnostic GNN framework (CAGNNs) to enhance the performance of most GNNs on heterophily datasets by learning the neighbor effect for each node. Specifically, we first decouple the feature of each node into the discriminative feature for downstream tasks and the aggregation feature for graph convolution. Then, we propose a shared mixer module to adaptively evaluate the neighbor effect of each node to incorporate the neighbor information. The proposed framework can be regarded as a plug-in component and is compatible with most GNNs. The experimental results over nine well-known benchmark datasets indicate that our framework can significantly improve performance, especially for the heterophily graphs. The average performance gain is 9.81%, 25.81%, and 20.61% compared with GIN, GAT, and GCN, respectively. Extensive ablation studies and robustness analysis further verify the effectiveness, robustness, and interpretability of our framework. Code is available at https://github.com/JC-202/CAGNN.
abstract_zh: 由于图卷积网络（GNN）中的同质假设，图节点分类任务中的一个普遍共识是，GNN 在同质图上表现良好，但在具有许多类间边的异质图上可能会失败。然而，之前的类间边缘视角和相关同质比指标无法很好地解释 GNN 在某些异质数据集下的性能，这意味着并非所有类间边缘都对 GNN 有害。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于冯诺依曼熵的新度量来重新审视 GNN 的异质性问题，并从整个邻居可识别的角度研究类间边缘的特征聚合。此外，我们提出了一个简单而有效的与卷积无关的 GNN 框架（CAGNN），通过学习每个节点的邻居效应来增强大多数 GNN 在异性数据集上的性能。具体来说，我们首先将每个节点的特征解耦为下游任务的判别特征和图卷积的聚合特征。然后，我们提出了一个共享混合器模块来自适应评估每个节点的邻居效应以合并邻居信息。所提出的框架可以被视为一个插件组件，并且与大多数 GNN 兼容。九个著名基准数据集的实验结果表明，我们的框架可以显着提高性能，特别是对于异质图。与 GIN、GAT 和 GCN 相比，平均性能增益分别为 9.81%、25.81% 和 20.61%。广泛的消融研究和稳健性分析进一步验证了我们框架的有效性、稳健性和可解释性。代码可在 https://github.com/JC-202/CAGNN 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为CAGNNs的卷积无关图神经网络框架，通过重新评估异质性图的异质性问题并学习节点邻居效应，提升GNN在异质性图上的分类性能。
AI.motivation: 传统GNN基于同质性假设，在异质性图（存在大量跨类边）中表现不佳。现有跨类边视角和同质性比例指标无法准确解释GNN在某些异质性数据集上的性能，表明并非所有跨类边对GNN有害。
AI.method: 提出基于冯·诺依曼熵的新异质性度量，从全邻居可识别视角分析跨类边特征聚合；设计CAGNNs框架，将节点特征解耦为任务判别特征和聚合特征，并通过共享混合模块自适应评估邻居效应。
AI.result: 在九个基准数据集上，CAGNNs相比GIN、GAT、GCN分别提升9.81%、25.81%、20.61%，消融实验和鲁棒性分析验证了框架的有效性与可解释性。
AI.conclusion: CAGNNs通过学习邻居效应有效提升了GNN在异质性图上的性能，且作为插件组件兼容多数GNN模型，为异质性图分析提供了新思路。

search_text: Exploiting Neighbor Effect: Conv-Agnostic GNNs Framework for Graphs with Heterophily 利用邻居效应：异质图的与卷积无关的 GNN 框架 Due to the homophily assumption in graph convolution networks (GNNs), a common consensus in the graph node classification task is that GNNs perform well on homophilic graphs but may fail on heterophilic graphs with many inter-class edges. However, the previous inter-class edges perspective and related homo-ratio metrics cannot well explain the GNNs performance under some heterophilic datasets, which implies that not all the inter-class edges are harmful to GNNs. In this work, we propose a new metric based on von Neumann entropy to re-examine the heterophily problem of GNNs and investigate the feature aggregation of inter-class edges from an entire neighbor identifiable perspective. Moreover, we propose a simple yet effective Conv-Agnostic GNN framework (CAGNNs) to enhance the performance of most GNNs on heterophily datasets by learning the neighbor effect for each node. Specifically, we first decouple the feature of each node into the discriminative feature for downstream tasks and the aggregation feature for graph convolution. Then, we propose a shared mixer module to adaptively evaluate the neighbor effect of each node to incorporate the neighbor information. The proposed framework can be regarded as a plug-in component and is compatible with most GNNs. The experimental results over nine well-known benchmark datasets indicate that our framework can significantly improve performance, especially for the heterophily graphs. The average performance gain is 9.81%, 25.81%, and 20.61% compared with GIN, GAT, and GCN, respectively. Extensive ablation studies and robustness analysis further verify the effectiveness, robustness, and interpretability of our framework. Code is available at https://github.com/JC-202/CAGNN. 由于图卷积网络（GNN）中的同质假设，图节点分类任务中的一个普遍共识是，GNN 在同质图上表现良好，但在具有许多类间边的异质图上可能会失败。然而，之前的类间边缘视角和相关同质比指标无法很好地解释 GNN 在某些异质数据集下的性能，这意味着并非所有类间边缘都对 GNN 有害。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于冯诺依曼熵的新度量来重新审视 GNN 的异质性问题，并从整个邻居可识别的角度研究类间边缘的特征聚合。此外，我们提出了一个简单而有效的与卷积无关的 GNN 框架（CAGNN），通过学习每个节点的邻居效应来增强大多数 GNN 在异性数据集上的性能。具体来说，我们首先将每个节点的特征解耦为下游任务的判别特征和图卷积的聚合特征。然后，我们提出了一个共享混合器模块来自适应评估每个节点的邻居效应以合并邻居信息。所提出的框架可以被视为一个插件组件，并且与大多数 GNN 兼容。九个著名基准数据集的实验结果表明，我们的框架可以显着提高性能，特别是对于异质图。与 GIN、GAT 和 GCN 相比，平均性能增益分别为 9.81%、25.81% 和 20.61%。广泛的消融研究和稳健性分析进一步验证了我们框架的有效性、稳健性和可解释性。代码可在 https://github.com/JC-202/CAGNN 获取。 本文提出一种名为CAGNNs的卷积无关图神经网络框架，通过重新评估异质性图的异质性问题并学习节点邻居效应，提升GNN在异质性图上的分类性能。 Jie Chen,Shouzhen Chen,Junbin Gao,Zengfeng Huang,Junping Zhang,Jian Pu cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Olivia Watkins,Trevor Darrell,Pieter Abbeel,Jacob Andreas,Abhishek Gupta
title: Teachable Reinforcement Learning via Advice Distillation
title_zh: 通过建议蒸馏进行可教学的强化学习
abstract: Training automated agents to complete complex tasks in interactive environments is challenging: reinforcement learning requires careful hand-engineering of reward functions, imitation learning requires specialized infrastructure and access to a human expert, and learning from intermediate forms of supervision (like binary preferences) is time-consuming and extracts little information from each human intervention. Can we overcome these challenges by building agents that learn from rich, interactive feedback instead? We propose a new supervision paradigm for interactive learning based on "teachable" decision-making systems that learn from structured advice provided by an external teacher. We begin by formalizing a class of human-in-the-loop decision making problems in which multiple forms of teacher-provided advice are available to a learner. We then describe a simple learning algorithm for these problems that first learns to interpret advice, then learns from advice to complete tasks even in the absence of human supervision. In puzzle-solving, navigation, and locomotion domains, we show that agents that learn from advice can acquire new skills with significantly less human supervision than standard reinforcement learning algorithms and often less than imitation learning.
summary: Training automated agents to complete complex tasks in interactive environments is challenging: reinforcement learning requires careful hand-engineering of reward functions, imitation learning requires specialized infrastructure and access to a human expert, and learning from intermediate forms of supervision (like binary preferences) is time-consuming and extracts little information from each human intervention. Can we overcome these challenges by building agents that learn from rich, interactive feedback instead? We propose a new supervision paradigm for interactive learning based on "teachable" decision-making systems that learn from structured advice provided by an external teacher. We begin by formalizing a class of human-in-the-loop decision making problems in which multiple forms of teacher-provided advice are available to a learner. We then describe a simple learning algorithm for these problems that first learns to interpret advice, then learns from advice to complete tasks even in the absence of human supervision. In puzzle-solving, navigation, and locomotion domains, we show that agents that learn from advice can acquire new skills with significantly less human supervision than standard reinforcement learning algorithms and often less than imitation learning.
abstract_zh: 训练自动化代理在交互式环境中完成复杂的任务具有挑战性：强化学习需要仔细手工设计奖励函数，模仿学习需要专门的基础设施和与人类专家的联系，而从中间形式的监督（如二元偏好）中学习非常耗时，并且从每次人工干预中提取的信息很少。我们能否通过构建从丰富的交互式反馈中学习的代理来克服这些挑战？我们提出了一种基于“可教学”决策系统的交互式学习新监督范式，该系统从外部教师提供的结构化建议中学习。我们首先将一类人机交互决策问题形式化，其中教师可以向学习者提供多种形式的建议。然后，我们描述了针对这些问题的简单学习算法，该算法首先学习解释建议，然后从建议中学习，即使在没有人类监督的情况下也能完成任务。在解谜、导航和运动领域，我们表明，从建议中学习的智能体可以在比标准强化学习算法更少的人类监督下获得新技能，而且通常比模仿学习更少。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的交互式学习监督范式，通过结构化建议（advice）让智能体在复杂任务中学习，减少对人类监督的依赖。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习需复杂奖励函数设计，模仿学习依赖专家示范且成本高，而基于偏好等弱监督的方法效率低下。需探索更高效的人机协作学习方式。
AI.method: 构建人类在环的决策框架，设计两阶段学习算法：先学习解析教师提供的结构化建议，再利用建议完成任务，无需持续人工干预。
AI.result: 在拼图、导航和运动控制任务中，基于建议学习的智能体相较标准强化学习和模仿学习，能以更少的人类监督获取新技能，且性能更优。
AI.conclusion: 建议蒸馏方法有效提升了交互式学习的效率，为减少人工监督的复杂任务学习提供了新思路，具有实际应用潜力。

authors_text: Martín Solís,Luis-Alexander Calvo-Valverde
title: Performance of Deep Learning models with transfer learning for multiple-step-ahead forecasts in monthly time series
title_zh: 具有迁移学习的深度学习模型在每月时间序列中进行多步预测的性能
abstract: Deep Learning and transfer learning models are being used to generate time series forecasts; however, there is scarce evidence about their performance prediction that it is more evident for monthly time series. The purpose of this paper is to compare Deep Learning models with transfer learning and without transfer learning and other traditional methods used for monthly forecasts to answer three questions about the suitability of Deep Learning and Transfer Learning to generate predictions of time series. Time series of M4 and M3 competitions were used for the experiments. The results suggest that deep learning models based on TCN, LSTM, and CNN with transfer learning tend to surpass the performance prediction of other traditional methods. On the other hand, TCN and LSTM, trained directly on the target time series, got similar or better performance than traditional methods for some forecast horizons.
summary: Deep Learning and transfer learning models are being used to generate time series forecasts; however, there is scarce evidence about their performance prediction that it is more evident for monthly time series. The purpose of this paper is to compare Deep Learning models with transfer learning and without transfer learning and other traditional methods used for monthly forecasts to answer three questions about the suitability of Deep Learning and Transfer Learning to generate predictions of time series. Time series of M4 and M3 competitions were used for the experiments. The results suggest that deep learning models based on TCN, LSTM, and CNN with transfer learning tend to surpass the performance prediction of other traditional methods. On the other hand, TCN and LSTM, trained directly on the target time series, got similar or better performance than traditional methods for some forecast horizons.
abstract_zh: 深度学习和迁移学习模型被用来生成时间序列预测；然而，很少有证据表明他们的绩效预测对于月度时间序列更为明显。本文的目的是比较带有迁移学习和不带有迁移学习的深度学习模型以及用于月度预测的其他传统方法，以回答有关深度学习和迁移学习是否适合生成时间序列预测的三个问题。 M4 和 M3 比赛的时间序列用于实验。结果表明，基于 TCN、LSTM 和 CNN 以及迁移学习的深度学习模型往往会超越其他传统方法的性能预测。另一方面，直接在目标时间序列上训练的 TCN 和 LSTM 在某些预测范围内获得了与传统方法相似或更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了深度学习模型（含迁移学习与不含迁移学习）与其他传统方法在月度时间序列多步预测中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏关于深度学习和迁移学习在月度时间序列预测中的性能证据，需验证其适用性。
AI.method: 使用M4和M3竞赛数据集，对比TCN、LSTM、CNN等深度学习模型（含/不含迁移学习）与传统方法的预测效果。
AI.result: 迁移学习的TCN、LSTM、CNN模型优于传统方法；直接训练的TCN和LSTM在部分预测范围表现与传统方法相当或更优。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习结合迁移学习可提升月度时间序列预测性能，但直接训练模型在特定场景仍具竞争力，需进一步探索模型优化。

authors_text: Mihir Prabhudesai,Anirudh Goyal,Sujoy Paul,Sjoerd van Steenkiste,Mehdi S. M. Sajjadi,Gaurav Aggarwal,Thomas Kipf,Deepak Pathak,Katerina Fragkiadaki
title: Test-time Adaptation with Slot-Centric Models
title_zh: 使用以槽为中心的模型进行测试时调整
comment: Accepted at ICML 2023. Project website at https://slot-tta.github.io/
comments: Accepted at ICML 2023. Project website at https://slot-tta.github.io/
abstract: Current visual detectors, though impressive within their training distribution, often fail to parse out-of-distribution scenes into their constituent entities. Recent test-time adaptation methods use auxiliary self-supervised losses to adapt the network parameters to each test example independently and have shown promising results towards generalization outside the training distribution for the task of image classification. In our work, we find evidence that these losses are insufficient for the task of scene decomposition, without also considering architectural inductive biases. Recent slot-centric generative models attempt to decompose scenes into entities in a self-supervised manner by reconstructing pixels. Drawing upon these two lines of work, we propose Slot-TTA, a semi-supervised slot-centric scene decomposition model that at test time is adapted per scene through gradient descent on reconstruction or cross-view synthesis objectives. We evaluate Slot-TTA across multiple input modalities, images or 3D point clouds, and show substantial out-of-distribution performance improvements against state-of-the-art supervised feed-forward detectors, and alternative test-time adaptation methods.
summary: Current visual detectors, though impressive within their training distribution, often fail to parse out-of-distribution scenes into their constituent entities. Recent test-time adaptation methods use auxiliary self-supervised losses to adapt the network parameters to each test example independently and have shown promising results towards generalization outside the training distribution for the task of image classification. In our work, we find evidence that these losses are insufficient for the task of scene decomposition, without also considering architectural inductive biases. Recent slot-centric generative models attempt to decompose scenes into entities in a self-supervised manner by reconstructing pixels. Drawing upon these two lines of work, we propose Slot-TTA, a semi-supervised slot-centric scene decomposition model that at test time is adapted per scene through gradient descent on reconstruction or cross-view synthesis objectives. We evaluate Slot-TTA across multiple input modalities, images or 3D point clouds, and show substantial out-of-distribution performance improvements against state-of-the-art supervised feed-forward detectors, and alternative test-time adaptation methods.
abstract_zh: 当前的视觉检测器虽然在其训练分布中令人印象深刻，但通常无法将分布外场景解析为其组成实体。最近的测试时间自适应方法使用辅助自监督损失来独立地使网络参数适应每个测试示例，并且在图像分类任务的训练分布之外的泛化方面显示出了有希望的结果。在我们的工作中，我们发现证据表明，在不考虑架构归纳偏差的情况下，这些损失不足以完成场景分解的任务。最近的以槽为中心的生成模型试图通过重建像素以自我监督的方式将场景分解为实体。基于这两条工作线，我们提出了 Slot-TTA，一种半监督的以槽为中心的场景分解模型，在测试时通过重建或跨视图合成目标的梯度下降来适应每个场景。我们跨多种输入模式、图像或 3D 点云评估 Slot-TTA，并显示相对于最先进的监督前馈检测器和替代测试时间适应方法的显着的分布外性能改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Slot-TTA方法，在测试时通过自监督重建或跨视图合成目标对槽中心模型进行适应，以提升分布外场景分解性能。
AI.motivation: 现有测试时适应方法在分类任务有效，但场景分解需结合架构归纳偏见与自监督学习，现有方法难以满足。
AI.method: 结合槽中心生成模型与测试时适应，通过梯度下降优化重建/跨视图合成目标，实现半监督场景分解。
AI.result: 在图像和3D点云模态下，Slot-TTA显著优于现有监督检测器及测试时适应方法，提升分布外性能。
AI.conclusion: Slot-TTA通过融合架构偏见与自监督适应，为分布外场景分解提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Christoph Mayer,Martin Danelljan,Goutam Bhat,Matthieu Paul,Danda Pani Paudel,Fisher Yu,Luc Van Gool
title: Transforming Model Prediction for Tracking
title_zh: 转换模型预测以进行跟踪
comment: Accepted at CVPR 2022. The code and trained models are available at https://github.com/visionml/pytracking
comments: Accepted at CVPR 2022. The code and trained models are available at https://github.com/visionml/pytracking
abstract: Optimization based tracking methods have been widely successful by integrating a target model prediction module, providing effective global reasoning by minimizing an objective function. While this inductive bias integrates valuable domain knowledge, it limits the expressivity of the tracking network. In this work, we therefore propose a tracker architecture employing a Transformer-based model prediction module. Transformers capture global relations with little inductive bias, allowing it to learn the prediction of more powerful target models. We further extend the model predictor to estimate a second set of weights that are applied for accurate bounding box regression. The resulting tracker relies on training and on test frame information in order to predict all weights transductively. We train the proposed tracker end-to-end and validate its performance by conducting comprehensive experiments on multiple tracking datasets. Our tracker sets a new state of the art on three benchmarks, achieving an AUC of 68.5% on the challenging LaSOT dataset.
summary: Optimization based tracking methods have been widely successful by integrating a target model prediction module, providing effective global reasoning by minimizing an objective function. While this inductive bias integrates valuable domain knowledge, it limits the expressivity of the tracking network. In this work, we therefore propose a tracker architecture employing a Transformer-based model prediction module. Transformers capture global relations with little inductive bias, allowing it to learn the prediction of more powerful target models. We further extend the model predictor to estimate a second set of weights that are applied for accurate bounding box regression. The resulting tracker relies on training and on test frame information in order to predict all weights transductively. We train the proposed tracker end-to-end and validate its performance by conducting comprehensive experiments on multiple tracking datasets. Our tracker sets a new state of the art on three benchmarks, achieving an AUC of 68.5% on the challenging LaSOT dataset.
abstract_zh: 基于优化的跟踪方法通过集成目标模型预测模块、通过最小化目标函数提供有效的全局推理而获得了广泛的成功。虽然这种归纳偏差整合了有价值的领域知识，但它限制了跟踪网络的表达能力。因此，在这项工作中，我们提出了一种采用基于 Transformer 的模型预测模块的跟踪器架构。 Transformer 以很少的归纳偏差捕获全局关系，使其能够学习更强大的目标模型的预测。我们进一步扩展模型预测器来估计用于精确边界框回归的第二组权重。由此产生的跟踪器依赖于训练和测试帧信息，以便以传导方式预测所有权重。我们端到端地训练所提出的跟踪器，并通过对多个跟踪数据集进行全面的实验来验证其性能。我们的跟踪器在三个基准上设定了新的最先进水平，在具有挑战性的 LaSOT 数据集上实现了 68.5% 的 AUC。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer的模型预测模块，用于改进目标跟踪任务中的网络表达能力，并通过端到端训练实现更精确的边界框回归。
AI.motivation: 传统优化跟踪方法依赖归纳偏置的模型预测模块，虽然有效但限制了网络表达能力，需更灵活的结构来提升性能。
AI.method: 设计Transformer-based模型预测模块替代传统结构，扩展预测器估计额外权重用于边界框回归，并通过端到端训练实现跨帧权重预测。
AI.result: 在多个跟踪数据集上达到新SOTA，尤其在LaSOT数据集上取得68.5%的AUC指标。
AI.conclusion: Transformer结构有效提升了模型预测模块的表达能力，验证了其在目标跟踪任务中的优越性。

authors_text: Matthieu Paul,Martin Danelljan,Christoph Mayer,Luc Van Gool
title: Robust Visual Tracking by Segmentation
title_zh: 通过分段进行稳健的视觉跟踪
comment: Accepted at ECCV 2022. Code and trained models are available at: https://github.com/visionml/pytracking
comments: Accepted at ECCV 2022. Code and trained models are available at: https://github.com/visionml/pytracking
abstract: Estimating the target extent poses a fundamental challenge in visual object tracking. Typically, trackers are box-centric and fully rely on a bounding box to define the target in the scene. In practice, objects often have complex shapes and are not aligned with the image axis. In these cases, bounding boxes do not provide an accurate description of the target and often contain a majority of background pixels. We propose a segmentation-centric tracking pipeline that not only produces a highly accurate segmentation mask, but also internally works with segmentation masks instead of bounding boxes. Thus, our tracker is able to better learn a target representation that clearly differentiates the target in the scene from background content. In order to achieve the necessary robustness for the challenging tracking scenario, we propose a separate instance localization component that is used to condition the segmentation decoder when producing the output mask. We infer a bounding box from the segmentation mask, validate our tracker on challenging tracking datasets and achieve the new state of the art on LaSOT with a success AUC score of 69.7%. Since most tracking datasets do not contain mask annotations, we cannot use them to evaluate predicted segmentation masks. Instead, we validate our segmentation quality on two popular video object segmentation datasets.
summary: Estimating the target extent poses a fundamental challenge in visual object tracking. Typically, trackers are box-centric and fully rely on a bounding box to define the target in the scene. In practice, objects often have complex shapes and are not aligned with the image axis. In these cases, bounding boxes do not provide an accurate description of the target and often contain a majority of background pixels. We propose a segmentation-centric tracking pipeline that not only produces a highly accurate segmentation mask, but also internally works with segmentation masks instead of bounding boxes. Thus, our tracker is able to better learn a target representation that clearly differentiates the target in the scene from background content. In order to achieve the necessary robustness for the challenging tracking scenario, we propose a separate instance localization component that is used to condition the segmentation decoder when producing the output mask. We infer a bounding box from the segmentation mask, validate our tracker on challenging tracking datasets and achieve the new state of the art on LaSOT with a success AUC score of 69.7%. Since most tracking datasets do not contain mask annotations, we cannot use them to evaluate predicted segmentation masks. Instead, we validate our segmentation quality on two popular video object segmentation datasets.
abstract_zh: 估计目标范围对视觉对象跟踪提出了根本性挑战。通常，跟踪器以框为中心，并完全依赖边界框来定义场景中的目标。在实践中，物体通常具有复杂的形状并且不与图像轴对齐。在这些情况下，边界框不能提供目标的准确描述，并且通常包含大部分背景像素。我们提出了一种以分割为中心的跟踪管道，它不仅产生高度准确的分割掩模，而且在内部使用分割掩模而不是边界框。因此，我们的跟踪器能够更好地学习目标表示，从而清楚地区分场景中的目标与背景内容。为了在具有挑战性的跟踪场景中实现必要的鲁棒性，我们提出了一个单独的实例定位组件，用于在生成输出掩模时调节分段解码器。我们从分割掩码推断出边界框，在具有挑战性的跟踪数据集上验证我们的跟踪器，并在 LaSOT 上实现新的最先进技术，成功的 AUC 分数为 69.7%。由于大多数跟踪数据集不包含掩模注释，因此我们无法使用它们来评估预测的分割掩模。相反，我们在两个流行的视频对象分割数据集上验证我们的分割质量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分割的鲁棒视觉跟踪方法，通过分割掩码代替传统边界框来更准确地描述目标。
AI.motivation: 传统视觉跟踪方法依赖边界框定义目标，但复杂形状目标常导致边界框包含大量背景像素，影响跟踪精度。
AI.method: 设计以分割为核心的跟踪流程，使用分割掩码替代边界框，并引入独立的实例定位组件辅助分割解码器生成更精确的掩码。
AI.result: 在LaSOT数据集上以69.7%的success AUC刷新了跟踪领域SOTA，分割质量在视频目标分割数据集上得到验证。
AI.conclusion: 分割掩码能更准确表征目标形态，结合实例定位组件的分割跟踪方法在复杂场景下表现出更优的鲁棒性和准确性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分割的鲁棒视觉跟踪方法，通过分割掩码代替传统边界框来更准确地描述目标。

ai_content.result: 在LaSOT数据集上以69.7%的success AUC刷新了跟踪领域SOTA，分割质量在视频目标分割数据集上得到验证。
ai_content.conclusion: 分割掩码能更准确表征目标形态，结合实例定位组件的分割跟踪方法在复杂场景下表现出更优的鲁棒性和准确性。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分割的鲁棒视觉跟踪方法，通过分割掩码代替传统边界框来更准确地描述目标。
motivation: 传统视觉跟踪方法依赖边界框定义目标，但复杂形状目标常导致边界框包含大量背景像素，影响跟踪精度。
method: 设计以分割为核心的跟踪流程，使用分割掩码替代边界框，并引入独立的实例定位组件辅助分割解码器生成更精确的掩码。
result: 在LaSOT数据集上以69.7%的success AUC刷新了跟踪领域SOTA，分割质量在视频目标分割数据集上得到验证。
conclusion: 分割掩码能更准确表征目标形态，结合实例定位组件的分割跟踪方法在复杂场景下表现出更优的鲁棒性和准确性。
search_text: Robust Visual Tracking by Segmentation 通过分段进行稳健的视觉跟踪 Estimating the target extent poses a fundamental challenge in visual object tracking. Typically, trackers are box-centric and fully rely on a bounding box to define the target in the scene. In practice, objects often have complex shapes and are not aligned with the image axis. In these cases, bounding boxes do not provide an accurate description of the target and often contain a majority of background pixels. We propose a segmentation-centric tracking pipeline that not only produces a highly accurate segmentation mask, but also internally works with segmentation masks instead of bounding boxes. Thus, our tracker is able to better learn a target representation that clearly differentiates the target in the scene from background content. In order to achieve the necessary robustness for the challenging tracking scenario, we propose a separate instance localization component that is used to condition the segmentation decoder when producing the output mask. We infer a bounding box from the segmentation mask, validate our tracker on challenging tracking datasets and achieve the new state of the art on LaSOT with a success AUC score of 69.7%. Since most tracking datasets do not contain mask annotations, we cannot use them to evaluate predicted segmentation masks. Instead, we validate our segmentation quality on two popular video object segmentation datasets. 估计目标范围对视觉对象跟踪提出了根本性挑战。通常，跟踪器以框为中心，并完全依赖边界框来定义场景中的目标。在实践中，物体通常具有复杂的形状并且不与图像轴对齐。在这些情况下，边界框不能提供目标的准确描述，并且通常包含大部分背景像素。我们提出了一种以分割为中心的跟踪管道，它不仅产生高度准确的分割掩模，而且在内部使用分割掩模而不是边界框。因此，我们的跟踪器能够更好地学习目标表示，从而清楚地区分场景中的目标与背景内容。为了在具有挑战性的跟踪场景中实现必要的鲁棒性，我们提出了一个单独的实例定位组件，用于在生成输出掩模时调节分段解码器。我们从分割掩码推断出边界框，在具有挑战性的跟踪数据集上验证我们的跟踪器，并在 LaSOT 上实现新的最先进技术，成功的 AUC 分数为 69.7%。由于大多数跟踪数据集不包含掩模注释，因此我们无法使用它们来评估预测的分割掩模。相反，我们在两个流行的视频对象分割数据集上验证我们的分割质量。 本文提出了一种基于分割的鲁棒视觉跟踪方法，通过分割掩码代替传统边界框来更准确地描述目标。 Matthieu Paul,Martin Danelljan,Christoph Mayer,Luc Van Gool cs.CV

authors_text: Nima Anari,Callum Burgess,Kevin Tian,Thuy-Duong Vuong
title: Quadratic Speedups in Parallel Sampling from Determinantal Distributions
title_zh: 行列式分布并行采样的二次加速
abstract: We study the problem of parallelizing sampling from distributions related to determinants: symmetric, nonsymmetric, and partition-constrained determinantal point processes, as well as planar perfect matchings. For these distributions, the partition function, a.k.a. the count, can be obtained via matrix determinants, a highly parallelizable computation; Csanky proved it is in NC. However, parallel counting does not automatically translate to parallel sampling, as classic reductions between the two are inherently sequential. We show that a nearly quadratic parallel speedup over sequential sampling can be achieved for all the aforementioned distributions. If the distribution is supported on subsets of size $k$ of a ground set, we show how to approximately produce a sample in $\widetilde{O}(k^{\frac{1}{2} + c})$ time with polynomially many processors for any constant $c>0$. In the two special cases of symmetric determinantal point processes and planar perfect matchings, our bound improves to $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt k)$ and we show how to sample exactly in these cases. As our main technical contribution, we fully characterize the limits of batching for the steps of sampling-to-counting reductions. We observe that only $O(1)$ steps can be batched together if we strive for exact sampling, even in the case of nonsymmetric determinantal point processes. However, we show that for approximate sampling, $\widetildeΩ(k^{\frac{1}{2}-c})$ steps can be batched together, for any entropically independent distribution, which includes all mentioned classes of determinantal point processes. Entropic independence and related notions have been the source of breakthroughs in Markov chain analysis in recent years, so we expect our framework to prove useful for distributions beyond those studied in this work.
summary: We study the problem of parallelizing sampling from distributions related to determinants: symmetric, nonsymmetric, and partition-constrained determinantal point processes, as well as planar perfect matchings. For these distributions, the partition function, a.k.a. the count, can be obtained via matrix determinants, a highly parallelizable computation; Csanky proved it is in NC. However, parallel counting does not automatically translate to parallel sampling, as classic reductions between the two are inherently sequential. We show that a nearly quadratic parallel speedup over sequential sampling can be achieved for all the aforementioned distributions. If the distribution is supported on subsets of size $k$ of a ground set, we show how to approximately produce a sample in $\widetilde{O}(k^{\frac{1}{2} + c})$ time with polynomially many processors for any constant $c>0$. In the two special cases of symmetric determinantal point processes and planar perfect matchings, our bound improves to $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt k)$ and we show how to sample exactly in these cases. As our main technical contribution, we fully characterize the limits of batching for the steps of sampling-to-counting reductions. We observe that only $O(1)$ steps can be batched together if we strive for exact sampling, even in the case of nonsymmetric determinantal point processes. However, we show that for approximate sampling, $\widetildeΩ(k^{\frac{1}{2}-c})$ steps can be batched together, for any entropically independent distribution, which includes all mentioned classes of determinantal point processes. Entropic independence and related notions have been the source of breakthroughs in Markov chain analysis in recent years, so we expect our framework to prove useful for distributions beyond those studied in this work.
abstract_zh: 我们研究从与行列式相关的分布中并行采样的问题：对称、非对称和分区约束的行列式点过程，以及平面完美匹配。对于这些分布，配分函数（又名计数）可以通过矩阵行列式获得，这是一种高度并行化的计算； Csanky 证明了它是在北卡罗来纳州。然而，并行计数不会自动转换为并行采样，因为两者之间的经典缩减本质上是顺序的。我们表明，对于所有上述分布，可以实现比顺序采样近乎二次的并行加速。如果在地面集的大小为 $k$ 的子集上支持分布，我们将展示如何在 $\widetilde{O}(k^{\frac{1}{2} + c})$ 时间内使用多项式多个处理器对任何常数 $c>0$ 近似生成样本。在对称行列式点过程和平面完美匹配的两种特殊情况下，我们的界限改进为 $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt k)$ 并且我们展示了如何在这些情况下精确采样。作为我们的主要技术贡献，我们充分描述了从采样到计数减少步骤的批处理限制。我们观察到，如果我们力求精确采样，即使在非对称行列式点过程的情况下，也只能将 $O(1)$ 个步骤分批在一起。然而，我们表明，对于近似采样，对于任何熵独立分布，包括所有提到的行列点过程类别，$\widetildeΩ(k^{\frac{1}{2}-c})$步骤可以一起批处理。近年来，熵独立性和相关概念一直是马尔可夫链分析突破的源泉，因此我们希望我们的框架能够证明对本工作中研究的分布之外的分布有用。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了如何并行化从行列式分布中采样的问题，包括对称、非对称行列式点过程及平面完美匹配等分布，实现了接近二次加速的并行采样方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管行列式分布的计数（分区函数）可通过矩阵行列式并行计算，但采样与计数的经典归约方法本身具有顺序性，限制了并行化潜力，因此需探索更高效的并行采样方法。
AI.method: 通过分析采样到计数归约步骤的批处理限制，结合熵独立性理论，提出针对近似采样的批处理策略，并设计适用于不同行列式分布的并行算法框架。
AI.result: 实现了所有研究分布的近似采样时间复杂度为~O(k^{1/2 + c})，对称行列式点过程及平面完美匹配可精确采样且时间复杂度为~O(√k)，并量化了近似采样下可批处理的步骤数量。
AI.conclusion: 本文框架通过熵独立性理论突破了精确采样的批处理限制，为行列式分布及其他相关分布的并行采样提供了通用方法，未来可扩展至更广泛的分布类别。

authors_text: Haotian Liu,Mu Cai,Yong Jae Lee
title: Masked Discrimination for Self-Supervised Learning on Point Clouds
title_zh: 点云上自监督学习的屏蔽歧视
abstract: Masked autoencoding has achieved great success for self-supervised learning in the image and language domains. However, mask based pretraining has yet to show benefits for point cloud understanding, likely due to standard backbones like PointNet being unable to properly handle the training versus testing distribution mismatch introduced by masking during training. In this paper, we bridge this gap by proposing a discriminative mask pretraining Transformer framework, MaskPoint}, for point clouds. Our key idea is to represent the point cloud as discrete occupancy values (1 if part of the point cloud; 0 if not), and perform simple binary classification between masked object points and sampled noise points as the proxy task. In this way, our approach is robust to the point sampling variance in point clouds, and facilitates learning rich representations. We evaluate our pretrained models across several downstream tasks, including 3D shape classification, segmentation, and real-word object detection, and demonstrate state-of-the-art results while achieving a significant pretraining speedup (e.g., 4.1x on ScanNet) compared to the prior state-of-the-art Transformer baseline. Code is available at https://github.com/haotian-liu/MaskPoint.
summary: Masked autoencoding has achieved great success for self-supervised learning in the image and language domains. However, mask based pretraining has yet to show benefits for point cloud understanding, likely due to standard backbones like PointNet being unable to properly handle the training versus testing distribution mismatch introduced by masking during training. In this paper, we bridge this gap by proposing a discriminative mask pretraining Transformer framework, MaskPoint}, for point clouds. Our key idea is to represent the point cloud as discrete occupancy values (1 if part of the point cloud; 0 if not), and perform simple binary classification between masked object points and sampled noise points as the proxy task. In this way, our approach is robust to the point sampling variance in point clouds, and facilitates learning rich representations. We evaluate our pretrained models across several downstream tasks, including 3D shape classification, segmentation, and real-word object detection, and demonstrate state-of-the-art results while achieving a significant pretraining speedup (e.g., 4.1x on ScanNet) compared to the prior state-of-the-art Transformer baseline. Code is available at https://github.com/haotian-liu/MaskPoint.
abstract_zh: 掩码自动编码在图像和语言领域的自监督学习中取得了巨大的成功。然而，基于掩模的预训练尚未显示出对点云理解的好处，这可能是由于像 PointNet 这样的标准骨干网无法正确处理训练期间掩模引入的训练与测试分布不匹配。在本文中，我们通过提出一种针对点云的判别性掩模预训练 Transformer 框架 MaskPoint} 来弥补这一差距。我们的关键思想是将点云表示为离散占用值（如果是点云的一部分则为 1；如果不是点云的一部分则为 0），并在屏蔽对象点和采样噪声点之间执行简单的二元分类作为代理任务。通过这种方式，我们的方法对点云中的点采样方差具有鲁棒性，并且有助于学习丰富的表示。我们评估了多个下游任务的预训练模型，包括 3D 形状分类、分割和真实物体检测，并展示了最先进的结果，同时与之前最先进的 Transformer 基线相比，实现了显着的预训练加速（例如，ScanNet 上的 4.1 倍）。代码可在 https://github.com/haotian-liu/MaskPoint 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MaskPoint的判别性掩码预训练Transformer框架，用于点云的自监督学习。
AI.motivation: 现有的基于掩码的预训练方法在点云理解中效果不佳，主要因为标准骨干网络（如PointNet）难以处理训练时掩码引入的分布不匹配问题。
AI.method: 将点云表示为离散的占用值（1表示点云部分，0表示噪声），通过二分类任务区分被掩码的物体点与采样噪声点，从而学习鲁棒的表示。
AI.result: 在3D形状分类、分割和现实物体检测等下游任务中取得SOTA结果，并在ScanNet上实现4.1倍的预训练加速。
AI.conclusion: MaskPoint方法有效解决了点云自监督学习的分布不匹配问题，同时显著提升了预训练效率。

search_text: Masked Discrimination for Self-Supervised Learning on Point Clouds 点云上自监督学习的屏蔽歧视 Masked autoencoding has achieved great success for self-supervised learning in the image and language domains. However, mask based pretraining has yet to show benefits for point cloud understanding, likely due to standard backbones like PointNet being unable to properly handle the training versus testing distribution mismatch introduced by masking during training. In this paper, we bridge this gap by proposing a discriminative mask pretraining Transformer framework, MaskPoint}, for point clouds. Our key idea is to represent the point cloud as discrete occupancy values (1 if part of the point cloud; 0 if not), and perform simple binary classification between masked object points and sampled noise points as the proxy task. In this way, our approach is robust to the point sampling variance in point clouds, and facilitates learning rich representations. We evaluate our pretrained models across several downstream tasks, including 3D shape classification, segmentation, and real-word object detection, and demonstrate state-of-the-art results while achieving a significant pretraining speedup (e.g., 4.1x on ScanNet) compared to the prior state-of-the-art Transformer baseline. Code is available at https://github.com/haotian-liu/MaskPoint. 掩码自动编码在图像和语言领域的自监督学习中取得了巨大的成功。然而，基于掩模的预训练尚未显示出对点云理解的好处，这可能是由于像 PointNet 这样的标准骨干网无法正确处理训练期间掩模引入的训练与测试分布不匹配。在本文中，我们通过提出一种针对点云的判别性掩模预训练 Transformer 框架 MaskPoint} 来弥补这一差距。我们的关键思想是将点云表示为离散占用值（如果是点云的一部分则为 1；如果不是点云的一部分则为 0），并在屏蔽对象点和采样噪声点之间执行简单的二元分类作为代理任务。通过这种方式，我们的方法对点云中的点采样方差具有鲁棒性，并且有助于学习丰富的表示。我们评估了多个下游任务的预训练模型，包括 3D 形状分类、分割和真实物体检测，并展示了最先进的结果，同时与之前最先进的 Transformer 基线相比，实现了显着的预训练加速（例如，ScanNet 上的 4.1 倍）。代码可在 https://github.com/haotian-liu/MaskPoint 获取。 本文提出了一种名为MaskPoint的判别性掩码预训练Transformer框架，用于点云的自监督学习。 Haotian Liu,Mu Cai,Yong Jae Lee cs.CV

authors_text: Nur Muhammad Shafiullah,Lerrel Pinto
title: One After Another: Learning Incremental Skills for a Changing World
title_zh: 一个又一个：为不断变化的世界学习增量技能
comment: To be published in The International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR) 2022
comments: To be published in The International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR) 2022
abstract: Reward-free, unsupervised discovery of skills is an attractive alternative to the bottleneck of hand-designing rewards in environments where task supervision is scarce or expensive. However, current skill pre-training methods, like many RL techniques, make a fundamental assumption - stationary environments during training. Traditional methods learn all their skills simultaneously, which makes it difficult for them to both quickly adapt to changes in the environment, and to not forget earlier skills after such adaptation. On the other hand, in an evolving or expanding environment, skill learning must be able to adapt fast to new environment situations while not forgetting previously learned skills. These two conditions make it difficult for classic skill discovery to do well in an evolving environment. In this work, we propose a new framework for skill discovery, where skills are learned one after another in an incremental fashion. This framework allows newly learned skills to adapt to new environment or agent dynamics, while the fixed old skills ensure the agent doesn't forget a learned skill. We demonstrate experimentally that in both evolving and static environments, incremental skills significantly outperform current state-of-the-art skill discovery methods on both skill quality and the ability to solve downstream tasks. Videos for learned skills and code are made public on https://notmahi.github.io/disk
summary: Reward-free, unsupervised discovery of skills is an attractive alternative to the bottleneck of hand-designing rewards in environments where task supervision is scarce or expensive. However, current skill pre-training methods, like many RL techniques, make a fundamental assumption - stationary environments during training. Traditional methods learn all their skills simultaneously, which makes it difficult for them to both quickly adapt to changes in the environment, and to not forget earlier skills after such adaptation. On the other hand, in an evolving or expanding environment, skill learning must be able to adapt fast to new environment situations while not forgetting previously learned skills. These two conditions make it difficult for classic skill discovery to do well in an evolving environment. In this work, we propose a new framework for skill discovery, where skills are learned one after another in an incremental fashion. This framework allows newly learned skills to adapt to new environment or agent dynamics, while the fixed old skills ensure the agent doesn't forget a learned skill. We demonstrate experimentally that in both evolving and static environments, incremental skills significantly outperform current state-of-the-art skill discovery methods on both skill quality and the ability to solve downstream tasks. Videos for learned skills and code are made public on https://notmahi.github.io/disk
abstract_zh: 在任务监督稀缺或昂贵的环境中，无奖励、无监督的技能发现是一种有吸引力的替代方案，可以替代手工设计奖励的瓶颈。然而，当前的技能预训练方法与许多强化学习技术一样，做出了一个基本假设——训练期间的静态环境。传统方法同时学习所有技能，这使得它们很难快速适应环境的变化，并且在适应后不会忘记以前的技能。另一方面，在不断发展或扩展的环境中，技能学习必须能够快速适应新的环境情况，同时不忘记以前学到的技能。这两个条件使得经典技能发现很难在不断变化的环境中发挥良好作用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的技能发现框架，其中以增量方式学习技能。该框架允许新学习的技能适应新的环境或代理动态，而固定的旧技能确保代理不会忘记学到的技能。我们通过实验证明，在不断发展的环境和静态环境中，增量技能在技能质量和解决下游任务的能力方面都显着优于当前最先进的技能发现方法。学习技能和代码的视频在 https://notmahi.github.io/disk 上公开

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种增量技能学习框架，使智能体在动态环境中逐步学习新技能而不遗忘旧技能。
AI.motivation: 传统技能预训练方法假设环境静态，难以适应动态环境变化且易遗忘已学技能，需解决环境演变中的快速适应与技能保留问题。
AI.method: 设计增量式技能学习框架，按序学习技能：新技能适应环境变化，旧技能保持固定以防止遗忘。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在动态和静态环境中均优于现有最先进的技能发现方法，技能质量及下游任务解决能力显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 增量技能学习框架有效平衡了动态环境适应性与技能保留性，为持续学习提供了新思路。

authors_text: Chethan M. Parameshwara,Gokul Hari,Cornelia Fermüller,Nitin J. Sanket,Yiannis Aloimonos
title: DiffPoseNet: Direct Differentiable Camera Pose Estimation
title_zh: DiffPoseNet：直接可微相机姿态估计
abstract: Current deep neural network approaches for camera pose estimation rely on scene structure for 3D motion estimation, but this decreases the robustness and thereby makes cross-dataset generalization difficult. In contrast, classical approaches to structure from motion estimate 3D motion utilizing optical flow and then compute depth. Their accuracy, however, depends strongly on the quality of the optical flow. To avoid this issue, direct methods have been proposed, which separate 3D motion from depth estimation but compute 3D motion using only image gradients in the form of normal flow. In this paper, we introduce a network NFlowNet, for normal flow estimation which is used to enforce robust and direct constraints. In particular, normal flow is used to estimate relative camera pose based on the cheirality (depth positivity) constraint. We achieve this by formulating the optimization problem as a differentiable cheirality layer, which allows for end-to-end learning of camera pose. We perform extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluation of the proposed DiffPoseNet's sensitivity to noise and its generalization across datasets. We compare our approach to existing state-of-the-art methods on KITTI, TartanAir, and TUM-RGBD datasets.
summary: Current deep neural network approaches for camera pose estimation rely on scene structure for 3D motion estimation, but this decreases the robustness and thereby makes cross-dataset generalization difficult. In contrast, classical approaches to structure from motion estimate 3D motion utilizing optical flow and then compute depth. Their accuracy, however, depends strongly on the quality of the optical flow. To avoid this issue, direct methods have been proposed, which separate 3D motion from depth estimation but compute 3D motion using only image gradients in the form of normal flow. In this paper, we introduce a network NFlowNet, for normal flow estimation which is used to enforce robust and direct constraints. In particular, normal flow is used to estimate relative camera pose based on the cheirality (depth positivity) constraint. We achieve this by formulating the optimization problem as a differentiable cheirality layer, which allows for end-to-end learning of camera pose. We perform extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluation of the proposed DiffPoseNet's sensitivity to noise and its generalization across datasets. We compare our approach to existing state-of-the-art methods on KITTI, TartanAir, and TUM-RGBD datasets.
abstract_zh: 当前用于相机姿态估计的深度神经网络方法依赖于场景结构来进行 3D 运动估计，但这降低了鲁棒性，从而使得跨数据集泛化变得困难。相比之下，经典的运动结构方法利用光流估计 3D 运动，然后计算深度。然而，它们的准确性在很大程度上取决于光流的质量。为了避免这个问题，人们提出了直接方法，将 3D 运动与深度估计分开，但仅使用法线流形式的图像梯度来计算 3D 运动。在本文中，我们引入了一个网络 NFlowNet，用于正常流量估计，用于强制执行鲁棒且直接的约束。特别是，法线流用于基于手性（深度正性）约束来估计相对相机姿态。我们通过将优化问题表述为可微分手性层来实现这一目标，该层允许对相机姿势进行端到端学习。我们对所提出的 DiffPoseNet 对噪声的敏感性及其跨数据集的泛化进行了广泛的定性和定量评估。我们将我们的方法与 KITTI、TartanAir 和 TUM-RGBD 数据集上现有的最先进方法进行比较。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DiffPoseNet的网络，用于直接进行相机姿态估计，该方法通过使用法线流和可微分的cheirality层来提升鲁棒性和跨数据集的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度学习方法依赖场景结构进行3D运动估计，这降低了鲁棒性并使跨数据集泛化变得困难。经典方法虽然利用光流估计3D运动，但其准确性高度依赖光流质量。为了解决这一问题，直接方法被提出，但需要改进鲁棒性。
AI.method: 本文引入了NFlowNet网络用于估计法线流，并通过可微分的cheirality层来直接估计相机姿态，从而实现端到端学习。
AI.result: 在KITTI、TartanAir和TUM-RGBD数据集上进行了大量定性和定量评估，表明DiffPoseNet在噪声敏感性和跨数据集泛化方面优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: DiffPoseNet通过法线流和可微分cheirality层实现了更鲁棒和直接的相机姿态估计，具有良好的跨数据集泛化能力。
